news.title: Over 45,000 Nashik properties in use without building completion documents
news.para: As per data available with the civic body, the highest number of unauthorised properties — around 10,400 — are located in the Nashik Road division, followed by Panchavati with 9,800 such properties.

news.title: About 3.76 lakh owners skip paying property tax, Nagpur civic body's coffers hit
news.para: For 2025-26, the Nagpur Municipal Corporation's dept initially set a target of Rs350 crore, which was later revised upwards to Rs400 crore, further widening the gap between expectations and actual collections.

news.title: Drone-based land mapping boosts rural credit flows
news.para: Drone-based land mapping under the SVAMITVA scheme has boosted rural credit by 23% by converting residential plots into verifiable collateral. This formalization of land ownership, replacing informal demarcation with property cards, reduces lender risk and enables banks to extend credit more freely to previously excluded rural households.

news.title: Dharavi redevelopment gathers pace; 11,000 rehab homes planned on railway land
news.para: The Dharavi Redevelopment Project is accelerating, and residents in Sector-6 on railway land have been urged to cooperate and vacate homes. Construction of new rehabilitation buildings will begin soon.  Residents have been offered transit accommodation or rental support.

news.title: Karnataka to develop private-style layouts for SC/ST communities
news.para: Under the scheme, fully serviced layouts with complete infrastructure will be created and residential sites allotted to people from Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) across the state.

news.title: About 19 big slum clusters across Mumbai set to undergo redevelopment under new scheme
news.para: Clusters of at least 50 acres, where over 51% of the area is occupied by slums, will be taken up for the projects. Even slums on coastal regulation zones I and II can be integrate

news.title: Haryana approves housing projects for 2,646 PMAY-Urban beneficiaries
news.para: Haryana government has approved housing projects for 2,646 beneficiaries across 60 urban local bodies under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U) 2.0. This initiative aims to provide affordable pucca houses to economically weaker sections, lower and middle-income groups. The state has seen significant public confidence with over 1.69 lakh registrations, and central funds have been released for thousands of homes.

news.title: Dubai realty shaken from West Asia conflict but holds its ground
news.para: Real estate transactions in Dubai fell 14% in early April amid the West Asia conflict, with buyer sentiment hit and rental demand sharply declining. Smaller developers offered discounts and flexible plans, while prices largely held. Though enquiries have picked up after a ceasefire, deals may take time. Experts say demand remains structurally strong, but prolonged tensions could trigger further price corrections and slower market activity.

news.title: PVC pipe maker Westlake agrees to pay $67 million to settle price-fixing lawsuit
news.para: The ⁠lawsuit, filed in 2024, alleges that Westlake and other major PVC pipe manufacturers conspired to ​fix prices charged to so-called direct purchasers and others in the United States beginning in early ​2020.

news.title: Nuvoco Vistas Corporation posts net profit of ₹359.77 crore in FY26
news.para: Nuvoco Vistas' PAT in Q4 FY26 was ₹140.81 crore, registering a dip of 14.94 per cent from ₹165.54 crore it witnessed in Q4 FY25. Its net consolidated total income stood at ₹3,309.37 crore in Q4 FY26 as against ₹3,046.57 crore it reported in similar quarter last year, registering a 8.63 per cent growth.

news.title: Mindspace REIT acquires 51% stake in ITPC - Radial Road for ₹3,000 crore
news.para: The acquisition will add ₹241 crore to net operating income (NOI) on a proforma basis (of which 51% is attributable to Mindspace REIT), while loan-to-value (LTV) ratio will increase from 28.0% to 30.3%.

news.title: China Evergrande liquidators enter exclusive talks to sell majority stake in property services arm
news.para: China Evergrande ​Group once embodied its name, rising to become the country's premier property developer and floating in Hong Kong in 2009 in the largest listing by a Chinese private developer.

news.title: Lucknow development body plans to ‘convert’ its low-rise buildings into modern high-rises
news.para: LDA Vice-chairperson Prathamesh Kumar on Monday directed officials to prepare detailed redevelopment proposals. In the first phase, plans will cover residential blocks on Wazir Hasan Road in Hazratganj, along with schemes in Paper Mill Colony and Sector-G of the Kanpur Road Scheme.

news.title: Listed hotel firms to add over 70,000 rooms by 2030: Report
news.para: India’s hospitality sector is entering a growth phase, with listed hotel operators expected to expand capacity significantly amid rising domestic travel, improving occupancies and increased investor interest.

news.title: Capacit’e, NCC bag ₹933 crore construction contracts from Great Value Realty, BPTP
news.para: Capacit’e Infraprojects and NCC have secured construction contracts worth over ₹900 crore from developers Great Value Realty and BPTP for residential projects in Noida and Gurugram.

news.title: Phoenix Mills: The workplace is no longer real estate; It is economic infrastructure
news.para: Phoenix Mills is redefining the modern workplace in India by developing integrated commercial ecosystems. Their Grade A office assets, embedded within mixed-use destinations, prioritize work, lifestyle, connectivity, and community. This strategic approach aims to enhance productivity, organizational culture, and long-term competitiveness for businesses and urban centers.

news.title: CBI searches in eight states in builders-financial institutions nexus case
news.para: The CBI conducted searches at 77 locations across eight states in a builders-financial institutions nexus case. This investigation, handed over by the Supreme Court, targets builders accused of duping homebuyers through subvention schemes and an alleged nexus with banks. The searches are part of ongoing efforts to address widespread fraud in real estate projects.

news.title: China Evergrande's founder pleads guilty to fraud in Shenzhen court
news.para: Hui Ka Yan and China Evergrande Group also face ​charges of illegally extending loans, fraudulently issuing securities and bribery by units, ⁠the the Shenzhen ‌Municipal Intermediate People's Court added, with verdicts to be handed down later, though it ​did not ​set a date.

news.title: Waste charges stall Bengaluru apartments' property tax payments
news.para: Bengaluru apartment dwellers are delaying property tax payments due to confusion over solid waste management charges.  Complexes generating over 100kg of waste daily are eligible for exemption but face a complex declaration process. Residents lack data on waste generation and fear penalties for incorrect submissions, urging authorities for clearer guidelines and a simplified system.

news.title: Indore civic body gets cracking on illegal colonies, 16 row houses razed
news.para: The crackdown primarily focused on Zone 4. In Anjani Nagar, four row houses illegally constructed near a drain were completely removed, while at Jagdish Nagar in Banganga area, removal operations targeted 12 row houses. Officials revealed that six of them were nearly complete with finishing work underway, while the remaining six were under construction.
